reasoning: GaussianInt is Zsqrtd (-1), with elements ⟨re, im⟩ where re, im : ℤ. A Gaussian integer with im = 0 is exactly the image of a rational integer under the canonical embedding ℤ → ℤ[i] (since Zsqrtd.ofInt n = ⟨n, 0⟩). The Lean statement asserts: for every nonzero ideal I, there exists z ∈ I (subtype element) with z ≠ 0 and (z : GaussianInt).im = 0. Since the subtype zero corresponds to the GaussianInt zero, this says I contains a nonzero element that is (the image of) a rational integer — precisely the informal claim. Quantifier structure, hypotheses, and conclusion match exactly.
